digests.narrative:
Photonics is his highest-conviction, highest-concentration theme, and the center of gravity has shifted hard onto the **CW-laser chokepoint**. The thesis: NVIDIA's EML bottleneck once forced an architectural shift that took Lumentum from ~$3B to $65B+ — and the same is repeating now with CW lasers + the 1.6T/CPO transition. The top-3 laser suppliers hold ~68% share and are sold out into 2028-2029, so AMD and other CSPs are signing LTAs hunting for the scraps.

His favored expression is **Sivers (SIVEF)** — a CW-laser chokepoint he's OSINT-mapped to an unusually broad set of hyperscaler programs (Jabil 1.6T LRO, POET/Lumilens, Ayar/NVLink, GlobalFoundries reference laser, O-Net, Marvell Celestial, likely Apple/AMD/Meta/Amazon), held as a million-plus share position into a 2027 volume ramp + NASDAQ listing. Around it: AAOI (~$471M/month revenue projections, 800%+ growth potential), plus JBL, LITE, COHR. He reads the recent selloff as rotation, not a broken thesis — 'markets rotate bottleneck to bottleneck' — and expects rotation back into photonics, with continued volatility until volume ramps.
keyPoints: The repeat trade: NVIDIA's CW-laser bottleneck + 1.6T/CPO mirrors the EML bottleneck that took LITE $3B → $65B+.
Top-3 laser suppliers ~68% share and sold out to 2028-2029; AMD/CSPs signing CW LTAs for the scraps.
Top pick SIVEF (Sivers): OSINT-mapped CW chokepoint across Jabil, POET, Ayar/NVLink, GFS, O-Net, Marvell; million-share hold into 2027 ramp + NASDAQ listing.
AAOI compelling on ~$471M/month projections (800%+ growth); JBL/LITE/COHR the supporting pluggable names.
Treats selloffs as rotation, not thesis breaks; expects rotation back into photonics (his highest-concentration theme).

digests.narrative:
Memory stays a 'massive bottleneck' and a core holding. HBM3e is shipping, HBM4 is in mass production, HBF is a 2027 story — all the same oligopoly (MU, SK Hynix, Samsung, Kioxia/Sandisk) re-rating mid-bottleneck and 'likely to keep compounding.' The market's focus rotated *into* memory this month (SNDK → MU) while photonics corrected.

He holds his Korea/memory exposure (EWY LEAPs up triple digits; the $DRAM ETF for broad SK Hynix/Samsung/MU/SNDK exposure) but isn't adding. Around it he hunts the upstream chokepoints — AXTI (InP substrates), the tungsten/WF6 supply scare into DRAM/NAND, and indium/MLCC export-control angles — and watches small-cap HBM4 hybrid-bonding suppliers like Auros (SK Hynix/Samsung; qualifying H2 → 2027 ramp).
keyPoints: 'Massive bottleneck': HBM3e shipping, HBM4 in mass production, HBF ~2027 — same oligopoly compounding.
Market focus rotated into memory this month (SNDK → MU) as photonics corrected.
Holds Korea/memory exposure (EWY triple-digit LEAPs, the $DRAM ETF) but isn't adding.
Upstream chokepoints: AXTI InP substrates, tungsten/WF6 and indium/MLCC export controls.
Watches small-cap HBM4 hybrid-bonding supplier Auros (SK Hynix/Samsung; H2 qualification → 2027 ramp).

digests.narrative:
Fabs are the derisked backbone. TSM is the centerpiece — building a silicon-photonics empire (COUPE/CoPoS via subsidiaries Xintec & VisEra), with 2-3yr volumes implying 2028 and pilot lines now, plus a 'sleeper' margin story (~$1.9T, ~2% uplift from price hikes) and autonomous/robotics as a surprise growth vector. INTC stays a 'great hold' (made-in-America foundry + EMIB advanced packaging); TSEM has re-rated hundreds of percent with more room — he'd 'pyramid' winners rather than take short-term gains.

He also mines the fab/inspection ecosystem for hidden gems — MSScorp (~$1.2B, a near-monopoly in CPO inspection serving TSM/NVDA/ASML/Intel), Xintech (TSM testing/COUPE), AP Memory. INTC even makes his '3 stocks to forget for 10 years' list (with RKLB and SIVE).
keyPoints: TSM building a silicon-photonics empire (COUPE/CoPoS via Xintec/VisEra); pilot lines now, volumes imply 2028.
TSM 'sleeper' ~$1.9T on ~2% price-hike margin uplift; autonomous/robotics a surprise growth vector.
INTC a 'great hold' (made-in-America foundry + EMIB); TSEM re-rated hundreds of % — 'pyramid,' don't take short-term gains.
Mines the ecosystem for hidden gems: MSScorp (~monopoly CPO inspection), Xintech (TSM/COUPE), AP Memory.
Backbone holds — more priced-in than photonics/memory.

digests.narrative:
One of his four repositioning themes, and strengthening. NBIS is the lead — he expects its $7-9B ARR to show at Q4 2026 earnings ('the big showtime'), and points to its $70 → $250+ recovery as the template ('dejavu') for his beaten-down photonics names.

A new structural tailwind: Google reportedly restricted Meta's compute in March 2026, pushing Meta into big neocloud deals (NBIS) — evidence that hyperscaler capacity is far below demand and they can't rely on each other, which he reads as bullish for the whole AI-datacenter buildout. Still a buildout phase — accumulate into the inflection rather than chase.
keyPoints: One of four repositioning themes; NBIS the lead — $7-9B ARR should surface at Q4 2026 earnings.
NBIS's $70 → $250+ recovery is his template ('dejavu') for the beaten-down photonics names.
New tailwind: Google restricting Meta's compute (Mar 2026) → Meta's NBIS deals → capacity far below demand.
Still buildout phase — accumulate into the inflection rather than chase.

digests.narrative:
His materials/packaging angle runs on one framework: equipment and machine suppliers book revenue *before* the volume ramp, because fabs need the machines first — which is why he's long LPK (LPK.DE). AEHR is the watch item: more priced-in after a 100% rally, but he expects volume orders soon to drive another ~30% leg.

Around that he tracks the upstream 'mini-shifts' — advanced-packaging and hybrid-bonding materials for memory, MLCC / tungsten (WF6) / indium export-control chokepoints, and substrate dynamics (Soitec / SOI.PA, framed through monopoly and licensing).
keyPoints: Framework: equipment/machine suppliers (LPK.DE) book revenue before the volume ramp — he's long LPK.
AEHR more priced-in after a 100% rally; expects volume orders soon → another ~30% leg.
Tracks upstream mini-shifts: advanced-packaging/hybrid-bonding, MLCC/WF6/indium chokepoints.
Substrate angle via Soitec (SOI.PA), framed through monopoly/licensing.

digests.narrative:
'Robotics is next' — deal count and investment at all-time highs (a16z/PitchBook). The new development: the first US pure-play humanoid is coming public — **Agility Robotics** (CCXI → AGLT, ~September IPO), which he's invested in. He favors application-specific humanoids (warehouses/delivery — $300M+ Digit v5 orders; Amazon/GXO/Toyota customers) over general-purpose plays like Figure, and frames Amazon as the clearest physical-AI hyperscaler (automating a ~1.57M workforce), with TSLA Optimus the broad-pitch proxy.

He still plays it largely through proxies and cross-exposure — AI-datacenter names carry humanoid exposure (memory for inference, DFB lasers for FMCW LiDAR) — ahead of a global humanoid IPO wave H2 into 2027.
keyPoints: 'Robotics is next' — deal count/investment at all-time highs (a16z/PitchBook).
First US pure-play humanoid IPO: Agility Robotics (CCXI → AGLT, ~Sept); he's invested — favors application-specific over general-purpose (Figure).
Amazon his clearest physical-AI hyperscaler (automating ~1.57M workforce); TSLA Optimus the broad proxy.
Cross-exposure play: AI-DC names carry humanoid exposure (memory, DFB lasers/LiDAR); global humanoid IPO wave H2 → 2027.

digests.narrative:
He treats NVDA (and AVGO) less as the trade than as a map to under-covered ecosystem suppliers — scanning Goldman ecosystem notes for the names NOT listed (the 'hidden gems'): MSScorp (CPO inspection ~monopoly) and Nextronics (~$200m, NVDA CPO connectors & cage thermal, also an Amazon/humanoid supplier). NVDA is also the upstream *cause* of the photonics trade — its LTAs are bottlenecking CW lasers and forcing architectural shifts.

New on his radar: Cerebras (CBRS) — OpenAI launched its 5.6 frontier model on Cerebras at ~750 tokens/sec; he took a starter 'cautionary' position ~$170 (below the $185 IPO) as tech validation, while noting it looks rich versus profitable names like JBL.
keyPoints: NVDA/AVGO used as a map to under-covered CPO ecosystem suppliers, not just the trade.
Method: scan Goldman ecosystem notes for names NOT listed — the 'hidden gems' (MSScorp, Nextronics).
NVDA's LTAs are the upstream cause bottlenecking CW lasers and forcing the photonics architectural shift.
New: starter position in Cerebras (CBRS) on OpenAI's 5.6 model at ~750 tok/s — 'cautionary,' looks rich vs JBL.
